info.title: ESignPay API
info.description:
API for managing document e-signatures, payments (Stripe 3D Secure), account opening with KYC/OCR verification, and OTP signing via Twilio SMS.

### Compression
All endpoints support **gzip compression**. Send `Accept-Encoding: gzip` to receive compressed responses (typically 60–80% payload reduction). Responses ≥ 256 bytes are automatically compressed.

### Range / Partial Content (206)
File download endpoints support **HTTP Range requests** (`Range: bytes=start-end`). The server responds with `206 Partial Content` and `Accept-Ranges: bytes`, enabling resumable downloads and partial fetches for large files.

servers.description: ESignPay API Server — gzip compression enabled on all endpoints

tags.name: Account Applications
tags.description: Account opening, KYC upload, OCR verification and contract generation

get.summary: Download document file (supports Range/206 partial content)
get.description: Supports HTTP Range header for partial content retrieval. Send `Range: bytes=start-end` to receive a 206 Partial Content response. The response includes `Accept-Ranges: bytes` to advertise range support.

parameters.name: If-None-Match
parameters.description: Previous ETag value. If it matches, server returns 304.

Accept-Ranges.description: Indicates the server supports byte-range requests

ETag.description: Entity tag for cache validation (MD5 of response body)

Content-Encoding.description: gzip when client sends Accept-Encoding: gzip

206.description: Partial Content — returned when a valid Range header is sent

Content-Range.description: Byte range of the returned content (e.g. bytes 0-99/1234)

ETag.description: Entity tag for cache validation (MD5 of response body)

416.description: Range Not Satisfiable — the requested range is invalid or beyond file size

ETag.description: Entity tag for cache validation (MD5 of response body)

304.description: Not Modified — returned when If-None-Match matches current ETag. No body is sent, saving bandwidth.

Content-Encoding.description: gzip when client sends Accept-Encoding: gzip
Compression algorithm used (gzip when Accept-Encoding: gzip is sent)

Vary.description: Indicates response varies based on Accept-Encoding

Accept-Ranges.description: Indicates that the server supports Range requests

Content-Range.description: Indicates the part of a document the server is returning (e.g. bytes 0-99/1234)

bearerAuth.description: Keycloak JWT Bearer token — obtain from POST to http://localhost:9090/realms/esignpayment/protocol/openid-connect/token
